source_quote: Symptom: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity after connecting wall or battery power. Likely cause: Blown input fuse F1 on the main power-input path, or no voltage reaching sense resistor R49. Components mentioned: F1, R49, J1. Rail / test point: Input rail (9–32 V nominal 24 V) measured at R49; fuse F1 in series before it.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally names F1 as the blown input fuse; F1 is in graph and bridge candidates.
Blown SMD fuse implies open-circuit failure mode.

evidence.source_quote_substring: No power at all — no LEDs, no rail activity
evidence.reasoning: F1 sources VIN per graph; loss of VIN cascades to all rails — symptom 'no power at all' confirms VIN is offline.

source_quote: Symptom: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. Components mentioned: U14, U18 (LPC11U24), D11. Rail / test point: LPC_VCC (3.3 V) at J22 pin 15; D11 illuminates when this rail is live.

evidence.reasoning: Dump literally names U14 as the failed regulator; matches bridge candidate U14.
Failed regulator with no output → dead.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U14 is source_refdes for LPC_VCC in the graph; killing U14 kills LPC_VCC.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U18 is powered by LPC_VCC; loss of LPC_VCC takes U18 offline.

source_quote: Symptom: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. Components mentioned: U14, U18 (LPC11U24), D11. Rail / test point: LPC_VCC (3.3 V) at J22 pin 15; D11 illuminates when this rail is live.

evidence.reasoning: Dump names U14 as the regulator whose output may be shorted; matches bridge candidate.
Shorted regulator output → shorted mode.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: Shorted U14 output shorts LPC_VCC to GND; U14 is source_refdes for LPC_VCC.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U18 loses supply when LPC_VCC is collapsed.

source_quote: Symptom: SD card boot fails; system won't read SD card even though LPC and 3.3 V main rail are confirmed live. Likely cause: 1.8 V rail dead — level shifters U28 and U8 require both 3.3 V and 1.8 V to operate, blocking the SD interface. Components mentioned: U28, U8 (level shifters). Rail / test point: 1.8 V rail at C130. Probe C130 for 1.8 V; if absent, identify 1.8 V buck regulator in schematic (Power section), check for short on output in diode-mode before reflowing or replacing.

evidence.reasoning: C130 is the 1.8V decoupling cap test point per dump and graph; matches bridge candidate.
Output short at the rail bypass cap is the leaky-short failure mode.
Leaky MLCC short typically presents as low-resistance path; 200 Ω chosen as typical leaky value.

evidence.source_quote_substring: level shifters U28 and U8 require both 3.3 V and 1.8 V to operate
evidence.reasoning: Loss of 1.8V kills U28 (and U8 — but U8 not in graph so only U28 listed).

source_quote: Symptom: Internal display dark or no video; USB ports also dead. External HDMI may or may not work. Likely cause: 1.2 V supply rail dead — both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate. Additionally, the display needs 3V3_PWR_AUX and USB_PWR (5 V) switched on by the System Controller. Components mentioned: U9 (TUSB8041), U10 (SN65DSI86), J24. Rail / test point: 1.2 V at C37; 3V3_PWR_AUX (3.3 V switched); USB_PWR (5 V, D12 indicator). Probe C37 for 1.2 V first; if absent, diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short.

evidence.reasoning: C37 is the 1.2V decoupling cap test point in dump and graph; matches bridge candidate.

evidence.source_quote_substring: diode-mode on nearby decoupling caps to detect short
evidence.reasoning: Decoupling cap short = leaky short failure.
Typical leaky MLCC presents low-ohm path; 200 Ω is a representative value.
C37 is decoupling cap of +1V2 rail in graph; leaky cap kills the 1.2V rail.

evidence.source_quote_substring: both the TUSB8041 USB hub (U9) and the SN65DSI86 eDP bridge (U10) require 1.2 V to operate
evidence.reasoning: U9 and U10 lose 1.2V supply when the rail collapses.

source_quote: Symptom: Internal display flickers intermittently or goes blank after reassembly; HDMI output unaffected. Likely cause: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable (30-pin, 0.5 mm pitch) between CPU module and SN65DSI86 eDP bridge, or Wi-Fi antenna routing interference into display path. Components mentioned: J24, U10 (SN65DSI86). Rail / test point: n/a (signal integrity issue, not a rail fault). Under microscope inspect J24 ZIF pads for lifted contacts; re-seat or re-flow.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Damaged or mis-seated J24 MIPI-DSI flex cable
evidence.reasoning: Dump literally names J24 as the damaged MIPI-DSI connector; matches bridge candidate J24.

evidence.source_quote_substring: inspect J24 ZIF pads for lifted contacts
evidence.reasoning: Lifted ZIF pads break signal continuity → open.
